summary: DHH briefly promotes his upcoming talk at the Reboot conference about how Ruby on Rails achieved outsized buzz through a combination of technology, necessity, and passion. He notes the talk will follow Jason Fried's presentation on small teams accomplishing big things. The post is essentially a conference talk teaser rather than a substantive argument, with DHH humorously acknowledging the challenge of following Jason's talk while competing with dinner.
key_points: Ruby on Rails' success came from equal parts technology, necessity, and passion
Developers driven by a cause are more productive than those without one
DHH and Jason Fried were already collaborating and cross-promoting at conferences in 2005
money_quotes: How Ruby on Rails used equal parts technology, necessity, and passion to create a larger-than-life buzz in web-application development.
Including why developers with a cause are more productive.
Quite the challenge following that and fighting a survival instinct to eat.
key_insight: Rails' explosive growth was driven not just by technical merit but by the passion and sense of mission behind it, making cause-driven developers more productive.
